question: Our band-gap dataset includes SiO2 at 8.9 eV, far above the others. Which summary value is pulled upward the most by this single outlier?

answers.feedback: Correct — the mean adds in every value, so one very large number drags it upward.
Not quite — the median is the middle value, so it barely moves when a single point is extreme.
Not quite — the mode is the most frequent value and ignores how large the outlier is.

question: A set of materials has a SMALL standard deviation in density. What does that tell you?

answers.answer: The densities are clustered close together.
answers.feedback: Correct — a small spread means the values sit near the mean.

answers.answer: The densities are spread far apart.
answers.feedback: That would be a large standard deviation.
Standard deviation measures spread, not the size of the average.
